findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 34. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The Roman numeral list items in the PDF use a more indented, structured layout with clear separation between the numeral and the content. In the HTML, the list items appear with less visual distinction in indentation hierarchy, though content is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF shows deeply indented sub-paragraphs under Roman numerals (e.g., IV) with clear visual nesting. HTML shows a flatter structure with less indentation distinction.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements create invalid HTML structure. The document has a <main> inside a <main>, which is semantically incorrect.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <main> as outer wrapper and then another <main> element wrapping the ordered list content.
